questions.question: Define a quadrilateral.
questions.answer: A quadrilateral is a closed figure with four sides and four vertices.

questions.question: State the sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral.
questions.answer: The sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral is 360°.

questions.question: State the sum of exterior angles of a quadrilateral.
questions.answer: The sum of exterior angles of a quadrilateral is 360°.

questions.question: Define a parallelogram.
questions.answer: A parallelogram is a quadrilateral with both pairs of opposite sides parallel.

questions.question: State the properties of a parallelogram.
questions.answer: Opposite sides are equal, opposite angles are equal, diagonals bisect each other.

questions.question: Define a rectangle.
questions.answer: A rectangle is a parallelogram with all angles 90°.

questions.question: State the properties of a rectangle.
questions.answer: Opposite sides are equal, diagonals are equal and bisect each other.

questions.question: Define a square.
questions.answer: A square is a rectangle with all sides equal.

questions.question: State the properties of a square.
questions.answer: All sides equal, all angles 90°, diagonals equal, bisect each other at right angles.

questions.question: Define a rhombus.
questions.answer: A rhombus is a parallelogram with all sides equal.

questions.question: State the properties of a rhombus.
questions.answer: All sides equal, opposite angles equal, diagonals bisect each other at right angles.

questions.question: Define a trapezium.
questions.answer: A trapezium is a quadrilateral with one pair of opposite sides parallel.

questions.question: Define a kite.
questions.answer: A kite is a quadrilateral with two distinct pairs of adjacent sides equal.

questions.question: State the property of diagonals in a kite.
questions.answer: One diagonal is the axis of symmetry and perpendicular to the other diagonal.

questions.question: State the property of diagonals in a rhombus.
questions.answer: Diagonals bisect each other at right angles and bisect opposite angles.

questions.question: State the property of diagonals in a rectangle.
questions.answer: Diagonals are equal and bisect each other.

questions.question: State the property of diagonals in a square.
questions.answer: Diagonals are equal, bisect each other at right angles, and bisect the angles of the square.

questions.question: State the property of diagonals in a parallelogram.
questions.answer: Diagonals bisect each other but are not necessarily equal.

questions.question: State the property of diagonals in a trapezium.
questions.answer: Diagonals are not generally equal and do not bisect each other.

questions.question: State a condition for a quadrilateral to be a parallelogram using sides.
questions.answer: If both pairs of opposite sides of a quadrilateral are equal, it is a parallelogram.

questions.question: State a condition for a quadrilateral to be a parallelogram using angles.
questions.answer: If both pairs of opposite angles are equal, the quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

questions.question: State a condition for a quadrilateral to be a rectangle.
questions.answer: If a parallelogram has one right angle, it is a rectangle.

questions.question: State a condition for a quadrilateral to be a rhombus.
questions.answer: If a parallelogram has diagonals perpendicular to each other, it is a rhombus.

questions.question: State a condition for a quadrilateral to be a square.
questions.answer: A quadrilateral which is both a rectangle and a rhombus is a square.

questions.question: State the condition for a quadrilateral to be a kite using sides.
questions.answer: If two distinct pairs of adjacent sides are equal, the quadrilateral is a kite.
